The United States Secret Service (USSS) was first established on July 5, 1865, as a branch of the U.S. Department of the Treasury. The original mission of the USSS was to combat counterfeiting, specifically to investigate and prevent the production and distribution of counterfeit currency.

The agency's primary focus was on enforcing the laws related to counterfeiting and protecting the integrity of the U.S. currency.

It wasn't until 1902, more than 35 years after its establishment, that the Secret Service began to provide protection for the President and his family, as well as visiting heads of state.
